reviews.review_description: you can definitely tell the bugs and its rough edges, but for an early access game its great! the environment is so imersive and so visually stunning. the combat could use some help though, you one tap enimies and they have these super annoying slow voices, what would be cool is a higher gore level (like nightclub sim VR) and add rocks that we can throw and bash peoples heads in. also the climbing is very buggy, but good for early access. the devs update it pretty frequently so i would 100% recommend cause it will be perfect in a few months

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!
It has been hard to keep working on this title (TBH), but we want to explore more this concept.
Would rly appreciate if you join our DIscord to give more feedback on the combat.
Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description: I hate to give this game 3 stars because this game has potential to be a very good vr game I think that the premise is solid but it needs some work on the main components. The gore is very bad and since I feel like this game is going for it they need to fix it. I would recommend looking at games like gorn, nightclub simulator, and battle talent. All these games have good gore and I believe it will help with that aspect of the game. Additionally I feel as though there needs to be waves or a tracker to show where enemies are because I found myself wandering the forest looking for them just to find nothing. Finally the enemies need guns and weapons as well and they should be able to take more damage before dieing. On the flip side this game has good graphics and good mechanics (climbing and picking up rocks and limb tearing) so as a final verdict I think this game has good potential saying that they are going to be adding most of what I just said but I do not recommend buying it at the moment and once the game gets better I will be more than happy to change my review
Needs a campaign and variety of enemies
This game is so fun it just needs some bug fixes and some better strength but so far its so fun

reviews.review_title: I see the potential, but needs work.
reviews.review_description:
I decided to give this a try despite it currently being in prototype (Early Access) form, as I’ve enjoyed other games by VRMonkey and was hopeful for this one too.

At the moment, it’s quite rough around the edges, but the potential is there. You play as a gorilla under attack from waves of humans, and must fight them off using your raw strength. You can smash, punch, and bash your enemies, and while it gets a little violent with dismemberments and decapitations, there isn’t much blood, which keeps it on the tamer side visually.

Controls currently include smooth locomotion and snap turning. I was very happy to see a crouch button included, as a disabled wheelchair user who relies on accessibility features to play VR games. That said, having additional options like height adjustment and smooth turning would be a big improvement.

I can see this becoming a lot more fun with more interactive elements and expanded gameplay. I'd love to see additional attack options like jumping, stomping, ripping and tearing limbs to use as weapons, throwing enemies, or even earning special attacks after building up kill streaks. Adding simple jungle weapons like sticks and rocks could also help mix up the combat and keep it engaging.

I expect this to be even more fun, like the other games from VR Monkey, in future updates.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the review! We are working on a roadmap to add more stuff in the game, but TBH we don't know if people likes the core gameplay.
Our idea is  to move further from the meme and become more like a run for freedom :D
Powers and skills are a must, for sure!

reviews.review_description: Spent a good time in it
I've only been playing this game for about 30 minutes, but it has ENORMOUS potential. So excited to see the final product good luck

You. are. literally a gorilla and move with joystick controls trying to defend and yourself from a bunch of dudes with no sense. It plays more like an arcade game where you try to beat your high score before. dying. To get points you punch, jab, karate chop a never ending wave of enemies. It's absolutely hilarious. One moment your ripping the head off a dude and the next your pulling limbs off a lifeless body. When your in the zone it feels a lot like playing a boxing game like les mills body combat. Uppercuts, jabs and hooks feel right and the quest tracks your arms perfectly. Graphics look great, the jungle environment totally works and the blood splatter is just perfect. With it still in early access there hope that more content is on the way and little imperfections get ironed out.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the feedback! TBH we didn't even consider that we would be confused by a GT clone before some bad feedback started to arise!
Hope you keep your eye in this game with the updates that are about to come!

reviews.review_description: Make a co op and get better physics and make different maps and make like a story mode you know what I’m saying and let us be able to sprint and jump let us craft a spear too but great game

developer_response.body: Right on track! Hope we can craft something sweet!
